summary: Since the last update, US-Iran diplomatic talks in Pakistan are ongoing amidst heightened Strait of Hormuz tensions with new Iranian threats. Israel and Lebanon are preparing for direct talks, signaling a potential shift in the Hezbollah conflict.

top_updates.headline: US clearing Strait of Hormuz, Iran threatens ships
IDF, Hezbollah trade strikes as Israel, Lebanon gear up for direct talks
US-Iran diplomatic talks in Pakistan continue through the night

detailed_analysis: Since the last update, US-Iran diplomatic engagement continues with ongoing talks in Pakistan, even as Strait of Hormuz tensions escalate due to explicit Iranian threats against ships. A significant development is Israel and Lebanon preparing for direct talks, offering a potential diplomatic track to de-escalate the Hezbollah conflict. Proxy actions by Iranian-backed militias in Iraq persist, exemplified by a recent kidnapping incident. No new nuclear developments are reported. Watch for outcomes of US-Iran and Israel-Lebanon talks, and any direct responses in the Strait.
